title: Fermented Snappy Algerian Rice Tagine
description: An adventurous Algerian dish featuring jasmine rice, yuzu-cure, and fermented flavors, suitable for a Buddhist temple-friendly meal in a wok.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: use a rice cooker if available

ingredients.name: yuzu juice
ingredients.notes: or substitute with lemon or lime

instructions: Heat a wok over medium-high heat.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Add 1 cup of jasmine rice and stir gently.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 18 minutes, or until rice is cooked.
While the rice cooks, combine yuzu juice, fish sauce, white vinegar, and brown sugar (if using) in a small bowl and mix well.
Add the yuzu mixture to the cooked rice and stir to combine.
Transfer the rice mixture to a fermentation vessel or a large glass jar with a lid.
Allow the mixture to ferment at room temperature for 2-3 days, or until slightly sour and effervescent.
Just before serving, heat a separate wok over medium heat.
Add chopped red bell pepper, green onions, and garlic, and stir-fry until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the fermented rice mixture and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Discard any remaining fermented rice mixture after serving.
